Hey team, I wanted to go over the leads and stats Google sheet that we provide to you. This is a sheet that works pretty cool. It has some nifty features and it's going to help you track your leads as they come in and then also mark what the status is on them. So if you already have a copy of this, we're going to give you a button below. When you click on that, it'll let you make a copy directly to your Google Drive. If you already have one, you don't need to make a copy of it. Once you get your sheet, it's probably going to be completely blank. I have a sample in here just so we can show you how it works. So it's just like Excel if you've used Excel before. The top, we have the name so you can click in here and rename it. You can move it to a different folder inside of your Google Drive if you want to. We have three different tabs on it at the bottom. We have tracking, we have stats, we have incoming leads. You're going to spend most of your time on the tracking page. Tracking is where you're able to mark whether or not somebody was a show, canceled, or no show. You're able to mark whether or not you made a sale and then the amount of that sale. There's also a notes column so that way you're able to mark any notes they asked you not to call or to call back later or they're going to reschedule or anything like that. The other page you're going to spend some time with is this stat sheet here. So this stat sheet tries to give you some automatic stats on how your system is going. It counts how many leads pop up on the sheet. It counts the cost per lead. It counts what the conversion rate is, so how many people are hitting that funnel versus how many people are actually getting through as a lead. And that also helps you track your scheduled, showed, canceled, no showed show rate, the number of sales you have, the percentage of sales out of your showed, the number of sales that you make out of your leads, and the amount of your sales, and then what your return on investment is. So on this stat sheet, all of it's automatic except for the ad spend and the unique clicks. You shouldn't need to make any changes to your stats at all. Ad spend is going to be from Facebook. It's the total amount that you spent. And then unique clicks is also one of the stats on Facebook ads. It's one of the columns that shows unique clicks. And that's going to be the number of people who actually clicked on it and it tries to remove any duplicates. The third tab down here, incoming leads, this is where your zap actually drops in the information. So you shouldn't normally need to make a change here, but if you speak with somebody later on and their email address was wrong or their phone number was wrong or their name was misspelled or they end up putting a duplicate, this is where you would fix that on this incoming leads. You want to try to avoid making changes. Just to make it towards a little bit harder to accidentally change something you don't want to. There are messages here. So if you go in here and try to change the date on this, for instance, you're going to see this heads up and it lets you know. You can cancel that out if you don't want to make the change or hit the OK if you do want to make the change. And so any information that comes into this incoming lead sheet, which is filled in by the zap, is going to pop up on this tracking sheet here. It's going to show the date that came through the first name, the last name, their email address, their phone number, when their appointment is. And that's all automatic. Everything else is something that you'll need to track every day. Now you want to check your lead sheet pretty regularly. I'd recommend at least twice a day, once in the morning, once in the evening. You can also do it more frequently. The same with your stats. You want to check on that at least once a day. You can check on it more if you want to. Now there are a couple things you want to make sure not to change on this leads and stat sheet. Make sure never to change the header rows. This row up here at the top, you don't want to change that, especially on the tracking, because it can cause things to break or not load up properly. You do have this undo and redo function. So if you ever make a change you didn't mean to make, you can undo it pretty quickly. So be sure to use this leads and stat sheet. Let us know if you have any questions or tech support rock stars are always happy to help with you. And I hope you really enjoy using this. It makes your jobs a little bit easier. Have a great rest of your day.
